figures.caption: Fig. 4: Subplots show the results of the ω optimization along different denoising steps for different class transitions. Both inbetween segments 2 and 3 show a similar pattern. ω peaks at denoising step 7 and then monotonically decreasing, consistently for all the class transitions 0→ 1, 0→ 5, 0→ 9.

Fig. 3: Subplots show generations of linear (first row), Sine (second row) and MDPA (last row) generated movement samples. The dash lined bounding box marks the transition phase. While the heuristic methods like linear and sine interpolation introduces additional folding of the charakter, the M-DPA shows more coherent transitions.

Fig. 2: Illustration of the guided denoising with additional control energy objective. Black line illustrations trajectory in latent space at t = T , red represents unconditional denoising trajectory with ϵθ(xt, t;∅) and the blue line with mixed guided denoising ϵθ(xt, t;ω). Ec is the control energy to be minimized for the guidance mechanisms to be close to the unguided trajectory.
Fig. 5: Evolution of control energy for both the Sine interpolation baseline and M-DPA.
